design_philosophy.core_loop: Select character with unique move set and fighting style → Engage in 1v1 match against opponent → Execute special moves, combos, and tactical positioning → Progress through arcade ladder
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Risk-reward balance between aggressive Custom Combos (high damage, vulnerable startup) vs. safe defensive play with Alpha Counters; character matchup knowledge vs. execution skill
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Master a diverse roster of iconic martial artists with distinct fighting philosophies; discover character-specific rival encounters and narrative conclusions through arcade mode progression
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Emphasis on normal hits and footsie-based gameplay rather than special move dominance (per Shinji Mikami design philosophy); Custom Combo system grants player expression over predetermined chains; character-specific story arcs and rival mechanics create narrative motivation beyond pure competition

difficulty.lives_system: Standard fighting game health bars per round; best-of-three match format
difficulty.continues_system: Arcade mode allows continues after loss
difficulty.checkpointing: Fixed 8-match arcade ladder with predetermined matchups and secret rival encounters triggered by performance milestones
difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Character selection directly impacts matchup difficulty; rival encounters conditional on meeting unspecified performance requirements, creating dynamic difficulty scaling; SNES version shows 'mixed results' in arcade-to-console translation suggesting difficulty balance issues in port

progression.upgrade_system: No traditional RPG upgrades; character progression through mastery of move sets; Super Combo gauge fills during combat and enables Custom Combos and Alpha Counters
progression.unlock_mechanism: Shin Akuma unlockable as secret character via high score (50,000+ points) and controller input sequence; character-specific rivals unlock mid-arcade-run via hidden conditions; alternative costume for Chun-Li (SF2-style) available
replayability_hooks: 16-character roster with distinct move sets and playstyles encourages multiple arcade runs
Character-specific rival encounters and unique ending sequences per character
Versus mode for head-to-head multiplayer competition
Secret Shin Akuma character with gameplay and visual distinctions
EX character variants in international arcade version (Evil Ryu, EX Dhalsim, EX Zangief) not in Japanese Zero 2

world_building_techniques: Pre-match and rival encounter dialogue exchanges establish character relationships and motivations
Character-specific arcade mode endings narrate individual story arcs (Ryu, Ken, Gen, Charlie, Akuma, Zangief had alternate ending tracks, though most replaced in SNES port)
Rival system creates narrative beats mid-ladder: rival interrupts standard match and exchanges dialogue before combat
Regional character name variations (Nash→Charlie, Gouki→Akuma, Sodom→Katana in international versions) reflect localization narrative choices

boss_design.philosophy: Fixed final opponent determined by player character selection; Shin Akuma serves as penultimate challenge before character-specific final boss rather than alternate ending boss; boss encounters tied to narrative rival system
notable_bosses: Shin Akuma (secret penultimate opponent) - enhanced version of Akuma character with altered visual presentation (grey gi → purple gi transformation), challenging power level distinct from regular Akuma
Character-specific final bosses (identity varies by player character selection)
Rival encounters (mid-arcade progression) - triggered by performance milestones, offer narrative exchanges before combat

faction_indicators: International vs. Japanese arcade version character rosters reflect regional player preferences (Capcom USA selected Evil Ryu and EX variants)
Character selection signals preferred fighting style archetype (grappler [Zangief], projectile zoner [Dhalsim], rushdown [Sakura], balanced [Ryu/Ken])
Rival system creates character loyalty narratives (e.g., Sakura as Ryu admirer, Gen as Akuma opponent)
danger_signals: Shin Akuma unlock condition (50,000+ arcade score) signals player commitment to mastery over casual play
Secret rival encounters require demonstrating above-average skill during ladder run
Custom Combo execution vs. reliance on basic attacks indicates mechanical skill gaps
environmental_storytelling: Stage selection tied to character identity (Dan's Hong Kong street market stage, Ryu/Sagat Australian outback special stage, M. Bison Venezuelan Angel Falls stage layout [unused in SNES])
Unused stage data (duplicate Australian outback with broken animations) indicates development resource constraints affecting SNES port quality
Long loading times between matches noted as significant console port limitation affecting player experience rhythm

sources_used: tcrf - Street Fighter Alpha 2 (SNES) - The Cutting Room Floor
